description: Australia vs UK 2025 gap vs CSO PEA18 ±2-3k precision band
notes: Gap +0.9k < precision band ±2.5k; tied within CI. Downgrade 'structural shift' to 'statistical tie; Aus at top in 2025 only'.
UK led by 9.9k (2023) and 4.6k (2024); Aus only ahead in 2025 (+0.9k). Paper's 'Since 2023 Australia has pulled ahead' is FALSE and must be rewritten.
69.9k → 65.6k (-6.1%). 2025 figure is PEA18 provisional and subject to revision; one-year dip does not by itself establish the wave has peaked.
Ireland remained NET-RECEIVING every year 2020-2025. Paper had +76k in the body — actual 2025 net is +59.7k (2024 was +79.3k).
Peak now annotated on emigration_trajectories.png and stated in paper §What we found.
11.1k across 23 unidentified countries rivals Aus/UK/EU14. Any single country inside Other-23 could rival top-4. Flagged as limitation in §What we cannot say.
Directory slug ie_graduate_emigration retained for history; paper §1 now states that graduate-specific analysis requires HEA GOS and is out of scope.
